questions.prompt: An athlete experiences a varus force on the proximal tibia. What type of fracture is he most likely to experience?

questions.category: Domain 1: Risk Reduction, Wellness and Health Literacy
questions.prompt: Why are children at a greater risk for heat-related illness?
choices: They have higher subcutaneous fat stores.
They have a reduced capacity to evaporate sweat.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are typical symptoms of someone suffering from acute stroke?
choices: numbness or weakness of the face, arm, or leg
confusion, speech problems, and cognitive defects

questions.prompt: Which of the following accurately describes fibromyalgia? Select all that apply.
choices: only occurs in patients with depression
responds well to most interventions with known analgesic effects
characterized by widespread muscle, joint, and connective tissue pain
occurs in the absence of tissue injury

questions.prompt:
Type: Matching drag drop

Match the pain with the structure and pathology.

zones.label: morning pain that decreases with movement
pain at night that is intractable
pain that occurs with walking but is relieved when seated
pain at rest and during activity

questions.prompt:
Type: Matching drag drop

Match the motor function.

During the evaluation of a patients knee injury the athletic trainer palpates the tibial tuberosity. On the image below, identify the tibial tuberosity.

Use your mouse and click/drag the circle to the area/region to be identified. You may reposition the circle until you are satisfied with its placement.

questions.title: Focused testlet
questions.scenario: A 70-year-old client presents to your clinic with shoulder pain that is worse at night. The client does not remember any trauma and reports insidious onset. Her AROM is limited in shoulder flexion and abduction. She has a positive painful arc and has weakness in abduction and external rotation.

questions.prompt: 1. What are the most appropriate special tests to perform. Select all that apply.

No change in symptoms is noted with the Spurling’s test, apprehension test, or crank test. The empty can test was positive.

2. Based on the noted shoulder weakness and positive special test, the most likely diagnosis is
3. You would like to start your patient on AAROM exercises for home. What are the most appropriate exercises for your patient to perform at home? Select all that apply.
choices: wall climbs using fingers to assist
bilateral shoulder flexion using a cane
